title: The Mona Lisa Vanished! And a Broadway Star Is Turning the Heist into a Musical
summary:
Have you ever wondered what would happen if the most famous painting in the world just vanished? Well, that's exactly what happened to the Mona Lisa in 1911. And now, Andrew Lloyd Webber — the guy who wrote super-famous musicals like 'The Phantom of the Opera' and 'Cats' — is writing a new musical about this wild true story!

Here's what went down: The Mona Lisa was hanging in the Louvre museum in Paris. A guy named Vincenzo Peruggia, who worked there as a glazier (someone who puts in glass), knew exactly how to steal it. He just put on a worker's smock (a kind of coat), took the painting off the wall, and walked right out! Nobody noticed for 28 hours.

Peruggia hid the painting in a trunk in his apartment for two years. He even talked to police at his house, and they didn't suspect him! Finally, he tried to get money for it in Italy, and the police caught him. He said he stole it because he thought it belonged in Italy.

When the painting was found, everyone went crazy for it. Before the theft, the Mona Lisa wasn't even the most famous painting in its room. After? It became a global superstar. Lloyd Webber's musical is going to tell this amazing story with songs and spectacle. I can't wait to see it!
why_it_matters: This story shows that unexpected events can make something much more famous, and that truth can be stranger than fiction – you never know when a surprising event might change how the world sees a piece of art.

keywords.explanation: The famous painting by Leonardo da Vinci that was stolen from the Louvre.
Disappeared completely, as when the painting was taken.
A play with songs that tells a story, like the one Andrew Lloyd Webber is writing.
The glazier who stole the Mona Lisa from the museum.
To take something without permission, as Peruggia did with the painting.
The authorities who later caught Peruggia after he tried to get money for the painting.

questions.question: Which musicals did Andrew Lloyd Webber write?
options: Only The Phantom of the Opera
Both The Phantom of the Opera and Cats
questions.correct_answer: Both The Phantom of the Opera and Cats

questions.question: How long did it take for anyone to notice the Mona Lisa was missing?
Where did Peruggia hide the painting?
Why did Peruggia say he stole the Mona Lisa?
options: Because he thought it belonged in Italy
questions.correct_answer: Because he thought it belonged in Italy

questions.question: What happened to the Mona Lisa after it was recovered?
Who is writing a musical about the Mona Lisa theft?

background_read: The Louvre in Paris is the world's largest museum and houses thousands of famous artworks.
Leonardo da Vinci painted the Mona Lisa in the early 1500s, and it is known for her mysterious smile.
Before the theft, the Mona Lisa was not as famous as it is today – the crime actually made it a worldwide icon.
Article_Structure: WHO: Vincenzo Peruggia, a glazier who worked at the Louvre, stole the painting.
WHAT: He took the Mona Lisa from the wall, hid it in a trunk for two years, and later tried to get money for it in Italy.
WHERE: The theft happened at the Louvre museum in Paris, and the painting was hidden in Peruggia's apartment.
WHY: Peruggia claimed he stole it because he thought the painting should be in Italy, its home country.

perspectives.description: The theft made the Mona Lisa world-famous, so more people now appreciate art and visit museums to see it.
Stealing is a crime that caused worry and loss for the museum, and it took years to recover the painting.
It's a fascinating true story that inspires new creative works, like Andrew Lloyd Webber's musical.
